name: Cris Cyborg

qualityWinLedger.context: Former UFC champion and legitimate five-round title challenger.
Unbeaten UFC contender who later challenged Amanda Nunes.
Experienced champion, discounted for UFC debut and division context.
Title challenger, discounted for UFC debut and shallow division.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: The Nunes loss did not end the window because Cyborg immediately re-proved elite UFC form against Spencer.

lossContextEvents.context: Beat unbeaten contender Felicia Spencer next.

shortCase: A compact UFC featherweight champion case: three title-fight wins, dominant control, a strong Holm defense, and one prime loss to Amanda Nunes.
bestArgument: Cyborg wins comparisons when championship substance and dominance outweigh deeper but less authoritative contender résumés.
counterArgument: Only seven UFC fights and one truly elite UFC win. Most of Cyborg’s broader greatness happened outside the scored scope.
whyRankedHere: Cyborg went 6-1, won the title, defended it twice, controlled most scored rounds, and lost only to Amanda Nunes.
whyNotHigher: Seven UFC fights, one truly elite UFC win, and shallow featherweight depth cap the case. Her extensive non-UFC greatness is not scored.
finalTakeaway: A legitimate upper-half women’s UFC legacy case: compact, dominant, and championship-proven.
